src_001.title: Receptor tyrosine-protein kinase erbB-2 (HER2/ERBB2)
key_findings: Primary sequence: 1255 amino acids
Domain structure: extracellular (23-653), transmembrane (654-676), intracellular (677-1255)
Functional annotation: receptor tyrosine kinase activity
Clinical significance: overexpressed in 20-30% breast cancers

src_002.title: HER2/ErbB2 extracellular domain in extended conformation in complex with trastuzumab antibody
key_findings: Recent high-resolution structure of HER2-trastuzumab complex
Extended conformation of HER2 ECD
Detailed antibody-antigen interface

src_003.title: Crystal structure of extracellular domain of human HER2 complexed with Herceptin Fab
key_findings: First detailed structure of trastuzumab-HER2 interface
Binding to juxtamembrane region (Domain IV)
Mechanism of dimerization inhibition

src_004.title: Cryo-EM structure of Her2 extracellular domain-Trastuzumab Fab-Pertuzumab Fab complex
key_findings: Dual antibody binding to HER2
Non-overlapping epitopes for trastuzumab and pertuzumab
Structural basis for combination therapy

src_005.title: HER2/ErbB2 extracellular domain in compact conformation in complex with trastuzumab antibody
key_findings: Compact conformation of HER2-trastuzumab complex
Conformational flexibility of HER2 ECD
Alternative binding mode analysis

src_006.title: Crystal structure of ErbB2 domains 1-3
key_findings: Activated conformation similar to ligand-bound EGFR
Structural insights into HER2 activation mechanism
Domain organization in extended state

src_007.title: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) clinical efficacy
key_findings: FDA approved for HER2+ breast cancer treatment
Mechanism: prevents HER2 dimerization and signaling
Target epitope in Domain IV (juxtamembrane region)
Proven therapeutic efficacy in multiple clinical trials

src_008.title: Pertuzumab (Perjeta) clinical efficacy
key_findings: FDA approved for HER2+ breast cancer (combination therapy)
Mechanism: prevents HER2-HER3 heterodimerization
Target epitope in Domain II (dimerization interface)
Synergistic effect with trastuzumab

src_009.title: HER2 conformational states analysis
key_findings: HER2 exhibits extended and compact conformations
Extended state more accessible for antibody binding
Conformational selection influences therapeutic efficacy

src_010.title: HER2-targeting antibody therapeutics
key_findings: Multiple FDA-approved HER2-targeting antibodies
IgG format most successful for HER2 targeting
Proven druggability of HER2 extracellular domain

src_011.title: Crystal Structure of the Kinase domain of Human HER2 (erbB2)
key_findings: High-resolution structure of HER2 kinase domain
Intracellular domain structure (not accessible for antibodies)
Reference for full-length HER2 modeling

src_012.title: HER2 extracellular domain structure-function
key_findings: Domain I and III: leucine-rich, potential ligand binding
Domain II: cysteine-rich, dimerization interface
Domain IV: cysteine-rich, primary antibody target region
Glycosylation important for proper folding

src_013.title: HER2-targeting antibody design principles
key_findings: Domain IV targeting most successful approach
Prevention of dimerization critical mechanism
IgG format preferred for half-life and efficacy
Fc engineering can enhance therapeutic effect

src_014.title: HER2 in cancer pathogenesis
key_findings: Overexpressed in 20-30% of breast cancers
Associated with aggressive tumor behavior
Key driver of cancer cell proliferation and survival
Validated therapeutic target with clinical precedent

src_015.title: EGF receptor family analysis
key_findings: HER2 lacks direct ligand binding capability
Forms heterodimers with other family members (HER1, HER3)
Constitutively active in overexpressing cancers
Unique among EGF receptor family members

structural_data.key_findings: Multiple high-quality structures confirm HER2 ECD architecture and antibody binding sites

clinical_validation.key_findings: Extensive clinical validation of HER2 as therapeutic target with multiple approved antibodies

sequence_and_domains.key_findings: Well-characterized protein sequence and domain organization

binding_mechanisms.key_findings: Clear understanding of successful antibody binding mechanisms and MOA

gaps_and_limitations: Precise atomic-level contact residues for hotspot definition require experimental validation
Effects of native glycosylation on antibody binding not fully characterized
Limited structural data on alternative conformational states
Detailed epitope mapping beyond trastuzumab/pertuzumab binding sites incomplete
